title: euphrasia collina var. striata
intro: Euphrasia collina var. striata, also known as the Mountain Eyebright, is a plant species within the Gentianaceae family, commonly found in mountainous regions of Europe. The plant contains bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, iridoids, and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Euphrasia collina var. striata have been traditionally used to treat eye infections, conjunctivitis, and other ocular conditions, as well as to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, are used to support eye health, boost the immune system, and provide relief from respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
